name: Resource Pack Status
desc: Client reports the resource pack load status to the server. Sent after S48PacketResourcePackSend.

fields.name: hash
fields.desc: SHA-1 hash of the resource pack (from S48)

notes: The server can force a resource pack. If the client declines, the server may kick the player.

implementation.overview: C19PacketResourcePackStatus reports the outcome of a resource pack download/load. Status values: SUCCESSFULLY_LOADED (0), DECLINED (1), FAILED_DOWNLOAD (2), ACCEPTED (3). AntiResourcePack modules intercept and either decline (send DECLINED) or fake-accept (send ACCEPTED then SUCCESSFULLY_LOADED) to avoid downloading resource packs while avoiding the server kick that follows DECLINED on forced-resource-pack servers.

modules.name: AntiResourcePack (Auto-Decline / Fake Accept)
modules.purpose: Avoid downloading resource packs by either declining them immediately or faking a successful download. On servers that force resource packs, declining may result in a kick — so the module sends ACCEPTED followed by SUCCESSFULLY_LOADED without actually downloading anything.
modules.how_it_works: When S48PacketResourcePackSend arrives, the module cancels the vanilla prompt and immediately sends C19 with ACCEPTED (to satisfy the server that the pack was accepted), then after a short delay sends SUCCESSFULLY_LOADED. The server never sends the actual pack data (S48 is cancelled or ignored) and the client never downloads anything, but the server thinks the pack was loaded successfully.
modules.vanilla_hook: NetHandlerPlayServer.processResourcePackStatus(C19PacketResourcePackStatus) — the server logs the status. Some servers use DECLINED as a kick trigger (forced resource packs). The server stores the hash of successfully loaded packs to avoid re-sending.
modules.anti_cheat_notes: Fake-accepting resource packs is not detectable by anti-cheats — the server cannot verify that the client actually downloaded the resource pack. However, on servers that use resource packs for anti-cheat payloads (custom textures for item detection), faking acceptance means the client won't receive the time-sensitive texture updates.

implementation.general_hooks:
Client: Vanilla NetHandlerPlayClient sends C19 when the user accepts or declines a pack. Rise 6.0 AntiResourcePack replaces the vanilla flow with fake ACCEPTED+SUCCESSFULLY_LOADED.

Server: NetHandlerPlayServer.processResourcePackStatus(C19PacketResourcePackStatus) — logs the status and may kick on DECLINED if resource-pack-is-required is set.
implementation.client_variations:
Rise 6.0: AntiResourcePack fake-accepts C19 with ACCEPTED+SUCCESSFULLY_LOADED. BlinkComponent exempts C19 from blink.
Gugustus: AntiResourcePack sends DECLINED to skip download.
Tenacity 6.0: AntiResourcePack has both 'Decline' and 'Fake Accept' modes.
